```python
import asyncio
import os
import json
import logging
import datetime
import re
from typing import Dict, Any, Optional
from pathlib import Path

from fastapi import FastAPI, Request, HTTPException
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse, FileResponse
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from pydantic import BaseModel

from promptchain import PromptChain
from promptchain.utils.agent_chain import AgentChain
from agents import DataAgent, UIDisplayAgent, RecorderAgent, NormalAgent

# --- Global Variables ---
agent_orchestrator: Optional[AgentChain] = None
app_logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None
log_dir: Optional[Path] = None

# --- Pydantic Models ---
class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
    message: str

class ActionRecord(BaseModel):
    action_type: str
    details: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    context: Dict[str, Any] = {}

# --- Logging Setup (Adapted from advanced_router_web_chat pattern for robustness) ---
def setup_logging():
    global app_logger, log_dir
    log_dir = Path("logs/ui_agent_project_logs")
    log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    log_file = log_dir / f"ui_agent_project_{timestamp}.log"

    root_logger = logging.getLogger()
    root_logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

    app_logger = logging.getLogger('ui_agent_project')
    app_logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

    console_handler = logging.StreamHandler()
    console_handler.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    console_formatter = logging.Formatter('%(levelname)s - %(name)s - %(message)s')
    console_handler.setFormatter(console_formatter)

    file_handler = logging.FileHandler(log_file)
    file_handler.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
    file_formatter = logging.Formatter(
        '%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - [%(filename)s:%(lineno)d] - %(name)s - %(message)s'
    )
    file_handler.setFormatter(file_formatter)

    if app_logger.hasHandlers():
        app_logger.handlers.clear()

    app_logger.addHandler(console_handler)
    app_logger.addHandler(file_handler)

    if file_handler not in root_logger.handlers:
        root_logger.addHandler(file_handler)

    logging.getLogger('httpx').setLevel(logging.WARNING)
    logging.getLogger('urllib3').setLevel(logging.WARNING)

    app_logger.info(f"Logging initialized. Log file: {log_file}")
    return app_logger

# --- Agent Initialization ---
async def initialize_agents():
    global agent_orchestrator, app_logger
    
    if not app_logger:
        app_logger = setup_logging()

    app_logger.info("Initializing agents for AgentChain...")

    try:
        # Initialize individual agents
        data_agent = DataAgent(models=["openai/gpt-4o-mini"])
        ui_agent = UIDisplayAgent(models=["openai/gpt-4o-mini"], ui_framework="bootstrap")
        recorder_agent = RecorderAgent(log_path="logs/actions_log.json")
        normal_agent = NormalAgent(data_agent, ui_agent, recorder_agent, models=["openai/gpt-4o-mini"])
        
        # Define agent descriptions for the router
        agent_descriptions = {
            "normal_agent": "Default agent for general conversation, demos, and routing medical requests.",
            "data_agent": "Specialized agent for extracting and structuring medical data.",
            "ui_agent": "Specialized agent for generating HTML UI components from medical data.",
            "recorder_agent": "Agent that logs user actions for audit purposes."
        }
        
        # Define the router prompt template for agent selection
        router_prompt_template = """
        Analyze the user's request and conversation history to choose the best agent to handle the request.
        Respond ONLY with a JSON object with one key, "chosen_agent", and the name of the agent.

        Available Agents:
        {agent_details}

        Conversation History:
        {history}

        User Request:
        "{user_input}"

        - If the user asks for a "demo", "example", "ui", or "test data", ALWAYS choose "normal_agent".
        - If the request is specifically about structuring or processing medical data, choose "data_agent".
        - For all other general conversation, choose "normal_agent".

        Example:
        User Request: "Show me a demo of the lab results UI"
        {{
        "chosen_agent": "normal_agent"
        }}

        Now, for the user request above, generate the JSON response.
        """
        
        # Set up the AgentChain orchestrator
        agent_orchestrator = AgentChain(
            router={
                "models": [{"name": "openai/gpt-4o-mini", "params": {"temperature": 0.1}}],
                "instructions": [None, "{input}"],
                "decision_prompt_templates": {
                    "single_agent_dispatch": router_prompt_template
                }
            },
            agents={
                "normal_agent": normal_agent,
                "data_agent": data_agent,
                "ui_agent": ui_agent,
                "recorder_agent": recorder_agent
            },
            agent_descriptions=agent_descriptions,
            execution_mode="router",
            verbose=True,
            auto_include_history=True,
            cache_config={
                "name": "ui_agent_session",
                "path": ".cache/",
                "include_all_instances": False
            }
        )

        app_logger.info("AgentChain orchestrator initialized successfully")
        
    except Exception as e:
        app_logger.error(f"Error initializing agents: {e}")
        raise

# --- FastAPI Application ---
app = FastAPI(title="UI Agent Project", description="Medical Agent System with AG-UI Support")

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["*"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

# Mount frontend files
current_dir = Path(__file__).parent
frontend_dir = current_dir / "frontend"
if frontend_dir.exists():
    app.mount("/frontend", StaticFiles(directory=frontend_dir), name="frontend")

@app.on_event("startup")
async def startup_event():
    await initialize_agents()

@app.on_event("shutdown")
async def shutdown_event():
    if app_logger:
        app_logger.info("Application shutting down")

# --- Frontend Serving Endpoint ---
@app.get("/")
async def get_index():
    frontend_file = current_dir / "frontend" / "vanilla.html"
    if frontend_file.exists():
        return FileResponse(frontend_file)
    else:
        return {"status": "running", "message": "AG-UI frontend not found"}

def extract_html_from_markdown(text: str) -> Optional[str]:
    """
    Extracts an HTML document from a string, designed to be robust.

    This function handles three primary cases for web UI generation:
    1.  The string is a Markdown code block with `html` specifier (e.g., ```html...```).
    2.  The string is a Markdown code block without a specifier (e.g., ```...```).
    3.  The string is the raw HTML document itself.

    It logs the outcome for easier debugging.

    Returns the cleaned HTML string or the original text if no block is found,
    assuming it might be raw HTML.
    """
    if not isinstance(text, str):
        return text

    # Regex to find ```html ... ``` or ``` ... ```
    # It's more forgiving about whitespace and the 'html' specifier.
    pattern = re.compile(r"```(?:html)?\s*([\s\S]+?)\s*```", re.DOTALL)
    match = pattern.search(text)

    if match:
        html_content = match.group(1).strip()
        if app_logger:
            app_logger.info("Successfully extracted HTML from markdown code block.")
        return html_content

    # If no markdown block is found, assume the text might be raw HTML,
    # but still log it for traceability.
    if app_logger:
        app_logger.info("No markdown code block found. Passing content as is (assuming raw HTML).")
    return text.strip()


# --- Manual AG-UI Agent Endpoint ---
@app.post("/manual_agent")
async def manual_agent_endpoint(request: dict):
    global agent_orchestrator, app_logger
    
    if not agent_orchestrator:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Agents not initialized")
    
    message = request.get("message", "")
    app_logger.info(f"Manual agent endpoint - message: {message[:100]}...")

    async def stream_generator():
        try:
            # 1. Get the raw response from the agent orchestrator
            app_logger.info("--- [1] Getting Raw Agent Response ---")
            raw_response = await agent_orchestrator.process_input(message)
            app_logger.debug(f"Raw Response: {str(raw_response)[:300]}...")

            # 2. The agent's final output is a JSON string, so we parse it
            app_logger.info("--- [2] Parsing JSON from Agent Response ---")
            
            agent_response_text = ""
            html_content = None
            
            try:
                response_data = json.loads(raw_response)
                agent_response_text = response_data.get("agent_response", "No conversational response.")
                
                # 3. Extract the raw UI output from the JSON
                app_logger.info("--- [3] Extracting Raw UI Output from JSON ---")
                ui_html_output = response_data.get("ui_html", "")
                
                if ui_html_output:
                    app_logger.debug(f"Raw UI Output: {ui_html_output[:300]}...")
                    # 4. Clean and extract the HTML from the UI output
                    app_logger.info("--- [4] Extracting and Cleaning HTML for Frontend ---")
                    html_content = extract_html_from_markdown(ui_html_output)
                    if html_content:
                        app_logger.debug(f"Cleaned HTML: {html_content[:300]}...")
                    else:
                        app_logger.warning("HTML extraction returned empty content.")
                else:
                    app_logger.info("No 'ui_html' content in agent response.")

            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                app_logger.warning("Response was not valid JSON. Treating as plain text.")
                agent_response_text = raw_response
                html_content = None
            except Exception as e:
                app_logger.error(f"An unexpected error occurred during response processing: {e}")
                agent_response_text = f"An error occurred: {e}"
                html_content = None

            # 5. Stream the conversational part first
            app_logger.info("--- [5] Streaming Final Content to Frontend ---")
            if agent_response_text:
                content_chunk = {"type": "content", "content": agent_response_text}
                app_logger.debug("Streaming: Conversational Text")
                yield f"data: {json.dumps(content_chunk)}\n\n"

            # 6. Stream the UI component separately, if it exists
            if html_content:
                ui_chunk = {"type": "ui_component", "content": html_content}
                app_logger.debug("Streaming: UI Component")
                yield f"data: {json.dumps(ui_chunk)}\n\n"
            
            # 7. Always send the end-of-stream signal
            app_logger.info("--- [6] Sending End-of-Stream Signal ---")
            end_chunk = {"type": "end"}
            yield f"data: {json.dumps(end_chunk)}\n\n"
            
        except Exception as e:
            app_logger.error(f"Error in manual agent endpoint: {e}")
            error_chunk = {"type": "content", "content": f"Error: {str(e)}"}
            yield f"data: {json.dumps(error_chunk)}\n\n"
            end_chunk = {"type": "end"}
            yield f"data: {json.dumps(end_chunk)}\n\n"

    return StreamingResponse(stream_generator(), media_type="text/event-stream")

# --- Action Recording Endpoint ---
@app.post("/api/record_action")
async def record_action_endpoint(action_data: ActionRecord):
    global agent_orchestrator
    
    recorder = agent_orchestrator.agents.get("recorder_agent") if agent_orchestrator else None
    if not recorder:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Recorder agent not initialized")
    
    try:
        action_dict = action_data.dict()
        recorded_action = recorder.record_action(action_dict)
        app_logger.info(f"Action recorded: {action_data.action_type}")
        return {"status": "ok", "action": recorded_action}
        
    except Exception as e:
        app_logger.error(f"Error recording action: {e}")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Error recording action: {str(e)}")

# --- Agent Status Endpoint ---
@app.get("/status")
async def get_status():
    if not agent_orchestrator:
        return {"status": "error", "message": "Agents not initialized"}
    
    return {
        "status": "running",
        "agents": list(agent_orchestrator.agents.keys())
    }

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import uvicorn
    import argparse

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run the UI Agent Project server")
    parser.add_argument("--silent", action="store_true", help="Run in silent mode with reduced logging")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Set logging level based on silent flag
    log_level = "INFO" if args.silent else "DEBUG"
    
    def find_available_port(base_port=8020, max_port=9000):
        import socket
        for port in range(base_port, max_port):
            try:
                with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
                    s.bind(('localhost', port))
                    return port
            except OSError:
                continue
        return base_port
    
    port = find_available_port()
    print(f"Starting UI Agent Project server on http://localhost:{port}")
    print(f"AG-UI Frontend available at http://localhost:{port}")
    
    # Set log_level in uvicorn
    uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=port, log_level=log_level.lower())
```

After you replace the file, please restart your server. The issue with the HTML not displaying should now be resolved.

          self.ui_generation_prompt = f"""
- You are a UI generation agent for a medical chat application. Given the following structured patient data, generate a complete, self-contained HTML document to be displayed in an iframe.
- 
- The HTML document MUST include:
- 1. A `<head>` section with CDN links for Bootstrap CSS and Chart.js.
- 2. A `<body>` with the main UI components.
- 3. Use Bootstrap classes for all styling.
- 4. For lab trends, output a `<canvas>` element with an appropriate ID.
- 5. Include a `<script>` block at the end of the body to render the Chart.js chart, including example data if real data is not available.
- 6. The entire output must be a single, valid HTML document.
+ You are a UI generation agent for a medical chat application. Your task is to generate a single, self-contained HTML `<div>` based on the structured patient data provided.
+ 
+ **IMPORTANT RULES:**
+ 1.  **Output only a `<div>` element.** Do NOT include `<html>`, `<head>`, or `<body>` tags.
+ 2.  Use Bootstrap classes for all styling (e.g., `card`, `list-group`, `alert`). The Bootstrap CSS is already loaded by the page that will display this `<div>`.
+ 3.  If a chart is needed for lab trends, create a `<canvas>` element with a unique ID inside the `<div>`.
+ 4.  Include a `<script>` tag *at the end of the `<div>`* to render the Chart.js chart. The Chart.js library is also pre-loaded on the parent page. Use example data if real data is not available.
+ 5.  The entire output must be a single HTML `<div>` block.
  
  Patient Data:
  {{structured_data}}
  
- Wrap the entire HTML output in a markdown code block like this:
+ Wrap the entire HTML `<div>` output in a markdown code block like this:
  ```html
- ... your HTML code ...
+ <div class="container-fluid">
+   ... your UI components go here ...
+ </div>
  ```
  """
```

<details>

*Called tool*: sequential-thinking - sequentialthinking: A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on, question, or revise previous insights as understanding deepens.

When to use this tool:
- Breaking down complex problems into steps
- Planning and design with room for revision
- Analysis that might need course correction
- Problems where the full scope might not be clear initially
- Problems that require a multi-step solution
- Tasks that need to maintain context over multiple steps
- Situations where irrelevant information needs to be filtered out

Key features:
- You can adjust total_thoughts up or down as you progress
- You can question or revise previous thoughts
- You can add more thoughts even after reaching what seemed like the end
- You can express uncertainty and explore alternative approaches
- Not every thought needs to build linearly - you can branch or backtrack
- Generates a solution hypothesis
- Verifies the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
- Repeats the process until satisfied
- Provides a correct answer

Parameters explained:
- thought: Your current thinking step, which can include:
* Regular analytical steps
* Revisions of previous thoughts
* Questions about previous decisions
* Realizations about needing more analysis
* Changes in approach
* Hypothesis generation
* Hypothesis verification

- total_thoughts: Current estimate of thoughts needed (can be adjusted up/down)
- is_revision: A boolean indicating if this thought revises previous thinking
- revises_thought: If is_revision is true, which thought number is being reconsidered
- branch_from_thought: If branching, which thought number is the branching point
- branch_id: Identifier for the current branch (if any)

8. Verify the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps

*Parameters*:
  * thought: **4. Final Diagnosis and Solution:**

The analysis confirms the backend is sending the correct data. The problem lies in the frontend JavaScript inside `vanilla.html`. The current logic tries to manage a single "AI message" element, updating it as new data chunks arrive. This is fragile and error-prone, as evidenced by the console errors in your screenshot. A JavaScript error likely occurs during stream processing, preventing the code block that appends the `iframe` from ever running.

The key issues are:
*   **Fragile State Management:** Relying on flags like `isFirstChunk` and appending to a single text variable (`fullResponseText`) makes the code complex and easy to break if data chunks arrive in an unexpected order or if parsing fails.
*   **Error Handling:** While a `try...catch` exists, an error might be happening outside of it or in a way that halts further processing of the stream.

**The Solution:**
I will refactor the frontend JavaScript to be more robust and stateless. Instead of updating a single placeholder, the new logic will:
1.  Remove the complex placeholder and spinner logic.
2.  Process each data chunk as it arrives.
3.  If it's a `content` chunk, create a **new, complete** "AI message" div and append it to the chat.
4.  If it's a `ui_component` chunk, create a **new, complete** `iframe` and append it to the chat.

This makes each part of the response independent, eliminating the risk of state management errors and ensuring that even if one part fails, the others can still render. I will now apply this fix.

_**Assistant**_

You are **100% correct**: these errors are due to malformed JSON chunks being sent to the frontend, which then tries to `JSON.parse` them and fails. This is a classic streaming bug, and it’s especially common when the backend sends large or multi-line content (like HTML with embedded `<script>` tags) that gets split across multiple TCP packets or chunks.

**What’s happening:**
- The backend is streaming JSON objects, but sometimes a chunk is split in the middle of a string or object.
- The frontend tries to parse each chunk as a complete JSON object, but if the chunk is incomplete, you get errors like `Unterminated string in JSON` or `Unexpected non-whitespace character after JSON`.

---

## Sequential Thinking: What’s the right fix?

### 1. **Backend:**
- The backend is correctly sending each JSON object as a single `yield` in the generator. But, due to how HTTP streaming works, a single `yield` can be split into multiple TCP packets, and the browser’s `read()` can return partial data.

### 2. **Frontend:**
- The frontend splits the stream by `data: ` and tries to `JSON.parse` each chunk. If a chunk is incomplete, this will fail.

### 3. **What’s the robust solution?**
- **Buffer the stream on the frontend.** Only try to parse a chunk when you have a complete JSON object.
- The best way is to ensure that each JSON object is sent as a single line, and the frontend splits on newlines (`\n\n` or `\n`), not just `data: `.
- Alternatively, catch the parse error, buffer the partial chunk, and try again when more data arrives.

---

## **How to Fix**

### **Frontend Fix (Recommended, Simple):**

Replace this:
```js
const jsonChunks = rawChunk.split('data: ').filter(c => c.trim());
for (const jsonChunk of jsonChunks) {
  try {
    const chunk = JSON.parse(jsonChunk);
    // ...
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error parsing chunk:', jsonChunk, error);
  }
}
```

**With a buffer approach:**
```js
let buffer = '';
while (true) {
  const { value, done } = await reader.read();
  if (done) break;

  buffer += decoder.decode(value);

  // Split on double newlines (end of SSE event)
  let parts = buffer.split('\n\n');
  buffer = parts.pop(); // Last part may be incomplete

  for (const part of parts) {
    if (!part.trim().startsWith('data: ')) continue;
    const jsonStr = part.replace(/^data: /, '').trim();
    try {
      const chunk = JSON.parse(jsonStr);
      // ... handle chunk ...
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error parsing chunk:', jsonStr, error);
    }
  }
}
```
- This way, you only try to parse a JSON object when you have a complete event.

---

### **Backend (Optional, for extra safety):**
- Make sure each `yield` ends with `\n\n` (which you already do).
- Never split a JSON object across multiple `yield`s.

---

## **Prompt Engineering (Secondary)**
- You are right: you should also instruct the agent to avoid generating triple backticks or any content that could break JSON encoding, but the main issue here is the streaming and chunking, not the prompt.

---

## **Summary Table**

| Problem                | Cause                        | Solution                        |
|------------------------|------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| JSON parse errors      | Incomplete JSON in chunk     | Buffer and parse only complete   |
| Unterminated string    | Chunk split in middle        | Buffer until full JSON received  |
| Unexpected character   | Not splitting on event end   | Split on `\n\n` (SSE event end)  |

---

**Find this section in your `<script>`:**
```javascript
const response = await fetch('/manual_agent', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({ message }),
});

const reader = response.body.getReader();
const decoder = new TextDecoder();
let isFirstChunk = true;

while (true) {
  const { value, done } = await reader.read();
  if (done) break;

  const rawChunk = decoder.decode(value);
  const jsonChunks = rawChunk.split('data: ').filter(c => c.trim());

  for (const jsonChunk of jsonChunks) {
    try {
      const chunk = JSON.parse(jsonChunk);

      if (isFirstChunk) {
        document.getElementById(spinnerId)?.remove();
        isFirstChunk = false;
      }

      if (chunk.type === 'content' && chunk.content) {
        addMessage('AI', chunk.content, true);
      } else if (chunk.type === 'ui_component' && chunk.content) {
        addUiComponent(chunk.content);
      } else if (chunk.type === 'end') {
        if (isFirstChunk) {
          document.getElementById(spinnerId)?.remove();
        }
        return;
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error parsing chunk:', jsonChunk, error);
    }
  }
}
```

**Replace it with this:**
```javascript
const response = await fetch('/manual_agent', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({ message }),
});

const reader = response.body.getReader();
const decoder = new TextDecoder();
let isFirstChunk = true;
let buffer = '';

while (true) {
  const { value, done } = await reader.read();
  if (done) break;

  buffer += decoder.decode(value);

  // Split on double newlines (end of SSE event)
  let parts = buffer.split('\n\n');
  buffer = parts.pop(); // Last part may be incomplete

  for (const part of parts) {
    if (!part.trim().startsWith('data: ')) continue;
    const jsonStr = part.replace(/^data: /, '').trim();
    try {
      const chunk = JSON.parse(jsonStr);

      if (isFirstChunk) {
        document.getElementById(spinnerId)?.remove();
        isFirstChunk = false;
      }

      if (chunk.type === 'content' && chunk.content) {
        addMessage('AI', chunk.content, true);
      } else if (chunk.type === 'ui_component' && chunk.content) {
        addUiComponent(chunk.content);
      } else if (chunk.type === 'end') {
        if (isFirstChunk) {
          document.getElementById(spinnerId)?.remove();
        }
        return;
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error parsing chunk:', jsonStr, error);
    }
  }
}
```

---

**This will make your frontend robust to chunked/partial JSON and eliminate the parsing errors.**  
No code blocks, no comments—just the exact replacement.
